{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39668014?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/0xHunSec", "id": 39668014, "login": "0xHunSec", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjM5NjY4MDE0", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48099", "cvss": {"score": 7.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}], "description": "### Impact
WsgiDAV 4.3.3 can allow a WebDAV request path containing an encoded parent-directory segment to escape the configured filesystem share root in a specific path layout.

### Patches
The issue is fixed with version 4.3.4.

### Preconditions

The practical impact depends on the deployment.

The deployment uses a filesystem-backed WsgiDAV share.

The attacker can send WebDAV requests accepted by that share. This may be an anonymous share or an authenticated WebDAV user. This is not an authentication bypass.

### Details

The issue is in `FilesystemProvider._loc_to_file_path()`. The method builds a candidate path with `os.path.abspath(os.path.join(root_path, *path_parts))`, then checks containment with `file_path.startswith(root_path)`. This is not path-boundary aware. For example, if the configured share root is `/tmp/share`, a resolved sibling path such as `/tmp/share_evil/secret.txt` still starts with the string `/tmp/share`.

In a local proof, this allowed GET, PUT, and DELETE requests to operate on files outside the configured share root.

The WSGI/server layer forwards the encoded dot segment to WsgiDAV's PATH_INFO. The local proof used `/%2e%2e/...`, which wsgiref passed through as `/../...`.

A sibling or neighboring path exists whose absolute path starts with the configured root path string, such as `/tmp/share` and `/tmp/share_evil`.

The WsgiDAV process has OS permissions for the outside path.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00072, "percentile": 0.22146}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-wxq4-cc2q-338q", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-11T20:28:45Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wxq4-cc2q-338q", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-wxq4-cc2q-338q"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48099"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-11T20:28:45Z", "references": ["https://github.com/mar10/wsgidav/security/advisories/GHSA-wxq4-cc2q-338q", "https://github.com/mar10/wsgidav/commit/f894ed8656d7bdd7438ab8148c5a02546cb15183", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wxq4-cc2q-338q"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/mar10/wsgidav/security-advisories/GHSA-wxq4-cc2q-338q", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/mar10/wsgidav", "summary": "WsgiDAV encoded dot segments can escape filesystem share roots", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-11T20:28:47Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-wxq4-cc2q-338q", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.3.4", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "wsgidav"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 4.3.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: WsgiDAV encoded dot segments can escape filesystem share roots
